mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction that suppresses corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expanding above its long-run trend level reflects robust manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency falls below 50% when industrial production exceeds the threshold during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 205 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are non-negative in greater than 65% of observations when the CPI threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) or forward returns turn negative in greater than 55% of cases when both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects strengthening aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to correlate with forward returns (Spearman rho < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal an imminent supply-side slowdown and reduced business investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below prior month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The signal generates positive forward returns in greater than 60% of triggered instances during contractionary periods.
